title: Languas wenzelii
intro: Languas wenzelii, also known as Wenzel's langsat, is a species of plant in the family Zingiberaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of the Philippines. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Languas wenzelii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Languas wenzelii is often used in traditional medicine and is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used to make herbal teas and tinctures.

This family includes a wide range of plants commonly known as gingers. The Zingiberaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, one of which is Alpinioideae, where Languas wenzelii is placed. The genus Languas is a group of tropical plants native to Southeast Asia. Languas wenzelii is a specific species within this genus, known for its unique characteristics and distribution.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
